item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ghost trick?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
anyone else who looked up scp-wiki.net/OMEGAZULUROSTER? no? just me?

ok ._. (great article tho)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am so happy that this scip fits well with author's vision. Liked it a lot.
I am pleased to be the 444th upvote.

item.title: Lol
item.content:encoded:
"Why has 05-8 stopped taking his medicine?"
Lol. It makes it seem like a parent lol. I'm imagining a ghostly entity arguing with 8 about his medication.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My brain has melted…

Each maitin bell, the Baron saith, knells us back to a world of death
— Samuel Taylor Coleridge

item.title: Re: :o
item.content:encoded:
The negative comments in the discussion only serve to prove that [DATA EXPUNGED]

Well at nearly +400, I can't really complain. You can't please everyone all the time!

item.title: :o
item.content:encoded:
This is brilliant. Easily one of my top10 favorite skips. I'm a sucker for infohazards. They are so inherently terrifying! Amazing work on putting this together too. The negative comments in the discussion only serve to prove that [DATA EXPUNGED]
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really hard read, but well worth the time investment. I know a lot of people might not like it, but I sure did.

Once you dig through the dense, complex presentation, the underlying narrative mirrors the core concept of the whole site. The Foundation does what they believe needs to be done, even if that means hurting and killing people who never deserved their fate, and they do it with pride and efficiency.

The best articles are the ones that make me question if we really are the "good guys". I only regret that I only have one +1 to give.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Been going on a deep dive through the antimemetics hub. This is gold.

Favorite detail: the footnotes showing the AM and CC divisions have come up with different terminology for the same phenomena

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Omae wa mou shindeiru.

Nani?

I quite enjoyed this one. Normally I'm a bit sick of format screws, but the way this was set up - the files of different divisions rather than "now read it again but with less redaction" - made it much more interesting to me. The complete lack of redaction is also a nice change of pace, and I loved how that ended up being explained by the "twist".

I feel like this is much less confusingly written than most format screws, as well. I read through before reading the declassified, and I came away with pretty much the same understanding as the declassified spells out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't practice Santería,
I don't have a crystal ball, and if I had a million dollars I'd spend it all.


Amos Sanchez, a Cubano-American, does practice Santería (IWT and access to Foundation funds might be considered adequate substitutes for crystal balls and a million dollars).

Ará Orún are the "saints" of Santería: guardian spirits that aren't direct ancestors.

I do have author page commentary, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could author please clarify what "Ará Orún" means?
I remember when I doubted I would have any articles above +100 and it wasn't that long ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one, but maybe that's because I just got done reading qntm's Antimimetics Division SCP and stories. Either way, I don't see it apparently being a "format screw" as a bad thing. If nothing else, it can serve as a source for inspiration for other writers who might figure out a way to blow everyone away with their style choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was actually unintentional, but hilarious. I named it Omega because death, and zero because it's a an MTF so secret that even the O5 might not know about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Omega-zero?

Wait, you named the Task Force made of ghosts "wooooooo?"

*glares in silent disapproval for several seconds, then upvotes*

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: http://www.wikidot.com/doc-modules:listpages

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded:
Still trying to understand the coding Sourcery you've put into this, any tutorials?

Otherwise, ignoring the cool format screw I found it an interesting SCP =P
and congrats on 200+ upvotes!
Hey this is now my second article to pass two centuries!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In order: yes, no, maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The office of O5-8 at Site-200

"Let me go!"

Contained a Type IX antimemetic trigger capable of inflicting severe retrograde amnesia.

Is this a reference to qntm's antimeme tale series?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found one of the stories first

Somebody has written stories about Omega-Zero?!?

Or do you mean qntmn's There is No Antimemetics Division series? This actually postdates all but the last two and isn't officially part of that series (which is a single-author work).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read this a little out of order(I found one of the stories first), but I didn't actually get the implications of READ THIS until I read the discussion pages.
I shouldn't have read it so late in the day. It was so creepy, I was really paranoid about the dark when I went to bed.
Upvoted for being one of the most disturbing things I've read yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly awesome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh yeah, I will do that.

ETA: Done.

ETA2: Yeah, I am not sure what tired me from last night was thinking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean this.

If you see a typographical error and want to fix it, you may find the appropriate page by entering "http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2111-*" as the URL where "*" is 1-5 (the warning page is 1, RED TALISMAN is 2… READ THIS is 5). NOTE: "Mnestics" is not a typo, it is the opposite of amnestics.

But yeah, I think it only helps people who want to translate the thing or those who feel the need to correct grammar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like with a comment? I don't think that is necessary anybody with a good reason to go to those pages can probably figure out how to or can ask. Otherwise I really would rather this stand as a single work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So something like this: http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-2111-*/noredirect/true

*=1-5

EDIT: Should the page source be updated so that people know where to go?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's one of the reasons why I wanted a way to filter them out of that table…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's because I put a redirect on them, you'll need to put "/noredirect/true" after the URL.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can go to his author page here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/sirpudding-s-author-page

Under Supplements, all the individual pages are there for you.

EDIT: Wait a minute, the supplements redirect to the main SCP page…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: User from Korean Wiki. How can I enter to the scp-2111-* pages? I tried to, for getting the page sources for translation, but every time the site just took me to the main page…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand that some people are slightly concerned with the whole 'format screw' thing, but I believe that this is actually extremely well formatted for such an endeavor. The layers help slowly to illustrate the depth that the article reaches, really helping to cement the last smack in the face: You're reading this from the inside, son.
I honestly didn't find the Michelle Yu story to be a conceit or too 'spoopy scawwy' for my tastes. I never felt that the immersion was broken, and never even suspected invented psycho-babble. Cheers, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the easter egg pointing towards what kicked off "We Need to Talk About Fifty-Five."

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Psst dcletus (and any other readers) only mods and admins can delete comments, and we only do so when they contain severe rule-breaking material. You're fine here. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was fast… didn't even have a chance to delete the comment! :X

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the opposite of amnestic and not a typo.

Read We Need to Talk about Fifty-Five.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is "mnestic" a typo or is that how you spell the opposite of amnestic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… I don't know if I could have written this without the layers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It recently occurred to me that the other reason that RED TALISMAN isn't Thaumiel is that personnel with level 2-4 clearance are supposed to be working with it, and they aren't cleared for the existence of Thaumiel objects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ridden Tongue

Finally, the cunnilingus SCP I've always wanted.

In the interest of non-shitpost crit, I'm gonna throw in with the "this story isn't particularly compelling to me" crowd, and add that while I don't think the format screw is gimmick-y per se, it is cruft that distracts from the core story with little benefit.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Yay, finally real +100!

technically it was at 101 (with deleted accounts subtracted) but I removed my vote for like two minutes so that counts right?

Seriously though, great job! Really happy to see this getting up there.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Eh, it's what the module says that matters IMO :)

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Although, there's four deleted account votes, so this really still at +99. :(

How did I miss one of my favorite SCPs reaching a hundred upvotes?

This was an absolute masterpiece, sirpudding. You deserve each and every one of these votes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There really isn't a digital plane here, unless you mean the level of reality where information entities can exist. Yu was originally an ordinary ghost (something like a preta) but by defining her in the database she was able to manifest there more fully (like a seance). By haunting the database, the Foundation's ghosts are able to manipulate information directly which allows them to be more conscious. This is kind of like how some versions of ghosts only seem to be aware when they are interacting with a haunted location.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, if I'm getting this right…

  • Mnestic treatment is a counter to amnestics.
  • Some of the people who knew Agent Yu before her death had underwent mnestic treatment, which somehow made their memories of her "more solid", to the point that it caused one of two anomalous effects: 1) preventing Agent Yu from passing on and turning her into a ghost in the "digital plane"; 2) Agent Yu's soul did really pass on, but an entity in the digital plane was created from the aforementioned memories and/or Agent Yu's own that genuinely believes itself to be Agent Yu.
  • Agent Yu went crazy for some reason (probably due to the total isolation) and started attacking people whose "artificially solid memories" were the cause of her predicament. Some of those she killed also became "trapped" like she was, and a good number of them converted to her cause for whatever reason.
  • The MTF Omega-0 guys did not join Agent Yu's followers because they were too loyal to the Foundation's cause, and also they avoided going insane like she did because, ironically, her actions meant they didn't have to suffer total isolation like she did.

Did I get anything wrong?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea here is that prior to manifesting in the database they weren't conscious informational constructs, as the database gives them a medium that is sophisticated enough to allow for memetic expression. They were either just dead or manifesting as more primitive ghosts that are unable to retain memory during that time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While no Ω-0 personnel are able to recall events between their deaths and 04/21/1981, it is theorized that SCP-2111 was attacking the memories of Foundation personnel during this time.

Here, Ω-0 personnel have no memory between Their death and the "Memetic Bomb" event, I guess Yu is not the only ghost, and it maybe some of the Ω-0 personnel are turned into ghost before "Memetic Bomb" event, so I guess that Yu have abilities to erase the memory of ghosts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, that seems pretty essentially correct.

Her target seems including another ghosts?

Not expressly prior to manifesting in the database, no. Though it is possible. What suggested this to you?

Also, I have moved the Chinese translation into our under constructing Chinese wikidot website:

Thanks! I'll change the link on my author page!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent work! Brilliant story, upvoted.

Here is my own explanation of this SCP, including time-line. Poor English, not sure is 100% correct, and translated form my reply on Chinese Site: http://scpfoundation.123ubb.com/t6117-topic#55988

Enjoy!

+ spoilers
- spoilers

0.Personal who undergoing a long term mnestic treatment will turn the dead who they remembered into a kind of information individual, a.k.a. ghost.

1. (10/23/1980)Agent Michelle Yu was KIA, and become a ghost.

2.(10/23/1980 - 04/21/1981)She request nothing but rest in peace, so that she attack those people who remember her by antimemetic and cognitohazard agent, attempt to erase the memory about her. Her target seems including another ghosts?<-Not correct

3.(04/21/1981)Counterconceptual Division, one of the two Foundation antimemetic divisions, has seek the threat about her and attempt to investigate it. However, when researcher Victoria Taylor upload her report to the position of SCP-2111, Agent Yu has edit the document, reform it into a "Memetic Bomb", filling with antimemetic and cognitohazard. Also, she let all the new ghosts will appear in the database of SCP-2111. Those "Memetic Bomb" keep appearing in SCP-2111's database since that day.

4.(04/21/1981 - 03/10/1994)Agent Yu, SCP-2111, raiding and killing multiple member of Counterconceptual and Antimemetic Division. Some of victim are joining her attack. They will leave the message with the ability of ghost. Their most threaten operation is try to erase the memory of O5-8, who in charge of antimemetic object.

5.(02/14/1984 - 03/10/1994)Researcher Amos Sanchez has been killed by Yu's gang, and reform into a ghost. He has fright against memetic and antimemetic before, so that he convene other ghosts and form a MTF, Omega-0. They developed a battle technique against information individual call Memetic Close Quarters Combat, MCQC Identity Warfare Training, IWT, and finally erased the existence of SCP-2111. Her followers has also erased, or rewrite into a member of Omege-0.

6.(03/10/1994 - today)For now, MTF Omege-0 is the third Foundation antimemetic divisions, which hidden in the world of death. They assisting another two divisions with IWT and telepathic ability, and fright against antimemetic individuals.

7.Three months later that (3.) happened(07/██/1981)，Antimemetic Division created a software name "RIDDEN TONGUE", to refining the Memetic Bomb in the database, and provided to Memetic Division as SCP object and cognitohazard resourses. Memetic Division use a software name "RED TALISMAN" to transform the resource into sorts of security memetic agent and weaponize media.

8.However, Counterconceptual Division doesn't know Antimemetic Division, and what they have done. They just saw Memetic Division using SCP-2111 to creating weaponize cognitohazard. So that they thought that Memetic Division has been permeated by SCP-2111, so that they developed "REVENANT THEORY", attempt to find the "spy" in Memetic Division. Since Agent Yu, the SCP-2111 what they finding has been killed by Omega-0, their work seem to be meaningless.

Conclusion: This is the brief history of Omega-0. Thank you, Agent Yu! Your pursue of rest provide a lot of useful security memetic agent for Foundation, amen :D

Also, I have moved the Chinese translation into our under constructing Chinese wikidot website: http://scp-wiki-cn.wikidot.com/scp-2111
I hope it can work well like original article :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is SCP-148 Euclid? RED TALISMAN isn't used to directly contain other skips, so it isn't a Thaumiel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been mentioned before, but I'm bringing it up again as I can't find the comment it originally came from:

I think the RED TALISMAN object class should be Thaumiel. My justification is that the Memetics Department is clearly benefitting from it and it can be used in the containment of other SCPs.

I still absolutely love this SCP, but this was one thing that sort of bugged me. Of course I'll leave it up to you, just though I'd remention it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't this more due to the fact that you were very young when she died?

I don't think so. My paternal grandfather died less than a decade ago, and even now he is has become a grandfather shaped blur in my memory. I remember his voice begin gravelly, but not really the sound of it, I remember things that he did, but mainly because I have told the story of those things, and the memories and the story of the memories have already conflated. This is how real human memory works, it fades and becomes conflated with imagined memory until it eventually becomes impossible to tell the difference. In the end all we have is the memory of memories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't this more due to the fact that you were very young when she died?
I was 12 when my paternal grandmother died. I can still hear her voice when she called me 'ma mienne' ("my own"). I can still see her face and the way she dressed.
I was 30 when my father died. I remember everything about him.
Same with my aunt (father's sister). I was 36 when she died.
Finishing grieving my father's death took years. I dreamed of him almost every night, and he was still alive, or alive again, saying with laughter in his voice: 'Naah, I decided not to be dead after all!' or other nice funny things. When I woke up from these dreams I frankly, honestly couldn't believe he was indeed gone. And the dreams were NOT nightmares: always very pleasant and positive… and realistic like hell. Yeah, grieving was a long effort! ;>
Sorry. This doesn't have much to do with critting your skip! ;P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And in the end, I still don't buy it. I have just downvoted.

No problem! I can't please everybody always. I really do think that a stripped down version that just started with "If you can read this you are already dead" wouldn't have been successful at all. That's a lot of buy-in to ask for up front for most readers!

However, I can still help with a few more typos I spotted ;>:

Thanks! I think I got them all!

I STRONGLY disagree with this.
For me, the grieving process is 'I don't want you to be dead. Come back. Or perhaps you can't come back, because you never left.'
The end of the grieving process is 'I accept that you are dead, but I will always remember and love you.'
Not sure it has an incidence on your SCP. But it might, just might explain in part why I can't 'get' into it?

+ What I mean is this:
– hide block


When I was a small child I loved my maternal grandmother. I was devoted to her. Now, 35 years after her death, I can't recall her face, or her voice, or what she smelled like, or anything she ever actually said to me, or really anything about her directly. I remember things that have been told to me, or that I have told myself; things that are really part of my personal narrative and not direct memories of her at all. I grieved her, and then ultimately forgot her. I didn't forget about her, but I did forget every memory that directly included her. Those memories existed, and now they don't.

If I was on mnestics from the age of five or so, this wouldn't be the case. I would have clear direct memories of her. Could I ever really have finished grieving?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Replying here so I have access to your arguments, instead of juggling between pages of the discussion.
Okay.
WHEW. You have given me a lot of work, Sirpudding!
And in the end, I still don't buy it. I have just downvoted.

+ show block
– hide block

Upon rereading the whole thing, I keep my opinion that it's overcomplicated with many details and twists which I personally find pointless.

However, I can still help with a few more typos I spotted ;>:

lethal cogitohazards

(page 1) should be 'cognitohazards'

cogitohazard program

(Revenant theory, 5th paragraph of description)

◾◾Whenever the living try to precieve our communications, it comes across laden with antimemetic amenstic effects at minimum, and is often cognitohazardous.

(Read this, in message from Amos Sanchez) (words in bold) Should be 'perceive' and 'amnestic'

I think that's it!

As a conclusion, one important (at least for me) thing:

It occurred to me that part of the grieving process is that we eventually forget the dead.

I STRONGLY disagree with this.
For me, the grieving process is 'I don't want you to be dead. Come back. Or perhaps you can't come back, because you never left.'
The end of the grieving process is 'I accept that you are dead, but I will always remember and love you.'
Not sure it has an incidence on your SCP. But it might, just might explain in part why I can't 'get' into it?
